proposition_text: The Claimant ("Meridian") is a public limited company and operates a chain of approximately 180 retail stores in the United Kingdom together with an online sales channel.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Raymond Okafor, Head of Retail Operations at Meridian Retail Group plc. I make this statement from my own knowledge save where otherwise indicated. I am responsible for the running of our store estate.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Meridian Retail Group plc.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote explicitly states that Meridian is a 'plc', which directly confirms the proposition's assertion that Meridian is a public limited company.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Meridian Retail Group plc – Master Services Agreement dated 14 March 2024 – NOTICE OF TERMINATION AND LETTER BEFORE ACTION
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Meridian Retail Group plc
classified_chunks.reason: The 'plc' designation in the company name directly confirms that Meridian is a public limited company, which is part of the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Helena Vance, Programme Director at Meridian Retail Group plc. I make this statement from my own knowledge, save where I indicate otherwise. I had overall responsibility for the Platform implementation programme on Meridian’s side.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Meridian Retail Group plc
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly confirms that Meridian is a public limited company, which is a key part of the proposition.

proposition_text: The Defendant ("TechFlow") is a limited company which designs, supplies and implements retail software systems.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: We act for TechFlow Solutions Limited. Your client’s claim is denied in its entirety. Liability and quantum are both disputed.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: TechFlow Solutions Limited
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly identifies the defendant as 'TechFlow Solutions Limited', which confirms the proposition that TechFlow is a limited company.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Priya Nair, Head of Procurement at Meridian Retail Group plc. I make this statement from my own knowledge. I was responsible for the contractual and commercial administration of the TechFlow contract.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the TechFlow contract
classified_chunks.reason: The quote only confirms the existence of a contract involving TechFlow, providing no information about TechFlow's legal structure or business activities.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Dr Alan Whitfield, BSc PhD CEng FBCS. I have 28 years’ experience in the design and assurance of enterprise retail IT systems. I am instructed by Caldwell Pryce LLP, solicitors for the Claimant, to give my independent opinion on (a) the availability of the Platform following go-live and (b) whether the Platform contained defects falling below the standard reasonably to be expected.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I have 28 years’ experience in the design and assurance of enterprise retail IT systems. I am instructed by Caldwell Pryce LLP, solicitors for the Claimant, to give my independent opinion on (a) the availability of the Platform following go-live and (b) whether the Platform contained defects falling below the standard reasonably to be expected.
classified_chunks.reason: The chunk discusses an expert's opinion on enterprise retail IT systems and a platform, which is the general subject area of the proposition, but it does not mention the Defendant ("TechFlow") or its specific business activities.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: We act for Meridian Retail Group plc ("our client"). We write in connection with your design, build and implementation of the inventory and EPOS platform under the above Agreement ("the MSA").
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: your design, build and implementation of the inventory and EPOS platform
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly states that the recipient (the defendant) performs design, build, and implementation of retail software systems, which directly supports the activities described in the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Raymond Okafor, Head of Retail Operations at Meridian Retail Group plc. I make this statement from my own knowledge save where otherwise indicated. I am responsible for the running of our store estate.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I am Raymond Okafor, Head of Retail Operations at Meridian Retail Group plc.
classified_chunks.reason: The quote identifies the witness and their role at a different company, providing no information about TechFlow's legal status or business activities.

proposition_text: By a written Master Services Agreement dated 14 March 2024 ("the MSA"), TechFlow agreed to design, build, configure and implement for Meridian a cloud-based inventory management and electronic point-of-sale platform ("the Platform").

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Meridian Retail Group plc – Master Services Agreement dated 14 March 2024 – NOTICE OF TERMINATION AND LETTER BEFORE ACTION
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Master Services Agreement dated 14 March 2024
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly confirms the existence and date of the Master Services Agreement as described in the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: We act for Meridian Retail Group plc ("our client"). We write in connection with your design, build and implementation of the inventory and EPOS platform under the above Agreement ("the MSA").
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: We write in connection with your design, build and implementation of the inventory and EPOS platform under the above Agreement ("the MSA").
classified_chunks.reason: The quote explicitly refers to an "MSA" under which the recipient (implicitly TechFlow) undertook the "design, build and implementation of the inventory and EPOS platform" for Meridian, directly confirming the existence of such an agreement and the services provided.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I signed the Master Services Agreement on Meridian’s behalf on 14 March 2024, and the related Order Form. The total contract value was £2.4 million.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I signed the Master Services Agreement on Meridian’s behalf on 14 March 2024
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly confirms the existence of a Master Services Agreement, its signing date, and Meridian's involvement, which are key factual assertions within the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: This certificate confirms that User Acceptance Testing of Phase 1 of the Platform (core inventory and EPOS) was carried out between 21 October and 11 November 2024. Meridian confirms that Phase 1 has passed User Acceptance Testing and is accepted, subject to the resolution of the outstanding minor (Severity 3 and 4) items listed in the snag log appended to the UAT report. No outstanding Severity 1 or Severity 2 items were recorded at the date of this certificate.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: User Acceptance Testing of Phase 1 of the Platform (core inventory and EPOS) was carried out between 21 October and 11 November 2024. Meridian confirms that Phase 1 has passed User Acceptance Testing and is accepted
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates that a platform matching the description in the proposition is being developed and accepted, which is consistent with an agreement to implement it, although it does not explicitly mention the Master Services Agreement or TechFlow's specific role.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The pleaded wasted expenditure of £1,800,000 is consistent with the Claimant’s accounting records and the sums invoiced and paid under the Master Services Agreement. I have no reason to challenge this element on a wasted-expenditure basis.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the sums invoiced and paid under the Master Services Agreement
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates that a Master Services Agreement existed and was acted upon, which is consistent with the proposition that such an agreement was made.

proposition_text: The MSA incorporated a Statement of Work ("the SOW") at Schedule 1, which set out the scope of the works, the implementation plan and the milestones.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The Supplier shall design, build, configure, test and implement the cloud-based inventory management and EPOS platform ("the Platform") in accordance with the Statement of Work set out at Schedule 1 (the "SOW").
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: in accordance with the Statement of Work set out at Schedule 1 (the "SOW")
classified_chunks.reason: The quote directly confirms that the Statement of Work, referred to as "the SOW," is set out at Schedule 1 and governs the execution of the works, thereby supporting its incorporation and role in defining the scope.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The go-live date of 1 October 2024 is a target date. The detailed implementation plan is a planning estimate and is subject to the change control procedure in clause 3 of the MSA.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The detailed implementation plan is a planning estimate and is subject to the change control procedure in clause 3 of the MSA.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote, originating from the Statement of Work, directly discusses the detailed implementation plan and its connection to the MSA, thereby supporting the proposition that the SOW set out the implementation plan and was incorporated into the MSA.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The Supplier shall use reasonable endeavours to achieve the target go-live date stated in the SOW. The parties acknowledge that the implementation timetable is a planning estimate and may be adjusted through the change control procedure.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The Supplier shall use reasonable endeavours to achieve the target go-live date stated in the SOW. The parties acknowledge that the implementation timetable is a planning estimate and may be adjusted through the change control procedure.
classified_chunks.reason: The quote refers to a 'target go-live date stated in the SOW' and an 'implementation timetable,' which is consistent with the SOW setting out an implementation plan and milestones.

classified_chunks.chunk_text:
Marcus,
Following the marketing review, the board wants a customer loyalty capability included in the platform before we go live — points accrual, redemption at the till, and a member database. I appreciate this was not in the original SOW, but it is now a priority for us.
Can you scope this as a change and let us have the cost and any timetable impact? We accept there may be a knock-on to the go-live date and we are willing to discuss a revised date to accommodate it.
Helena
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I appreciate this was not in the original SOW
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates the existence of an 'original SOW' that defined the scope of work, which is consistent with the proposition that an SOW existed and set out the scope of works.

proposition_text: The total charges payable under the MSA were £2,400,000 (excluding VAT), payable against milestones.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The total charges for the Services are £2,400,000 (excluding VAT), payable against the milestones set out in the SOW and as may be varied by agreement in writing.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The total charges for the Services are £2,400,000 (excluding VAT), payable against the milestones set out in the SOW and as may be varied by agreement in writing.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly states the total charges and the payment method, which precisely matches the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The additional charge for the Loyalty Module is £180,000, payable on go-live. Agreed and signed by the parties:
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The additional charge for the Loyalty Module is £180,000, payable on go-live.
classified_chunks.reason: The chunk specifies an additional charge and its payment trigger, but it does not confirm or deny the total charges or the general payment against milestones as stated in the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: With effect from the date of this Deed, clause 4.1 of the SOW is varied so that the charges are payable: 20% on M1, 20% on M2, 30% on M3 and 30% on M4.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the charges are payable: 20% on M1, 20% on M2, 30% on M3 and 30% on M4.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote explicitly states that charges are payable against specific milestones, directly confirming the 'payable against milestones' aspect of the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The pleaded wasted expenditure of £1,800,000 is consistent with the Claimant’s accounting records and the sums invoiced and paid under the Master Services Agreement. I have no reason to challenge this element on a wasted-expenditure basis.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The pleaded wasted expenditure of £1,800,000 is consistent with the Claimant’s accounting records and the sums invoiced and paid under the Master Services Agreement.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote confirms that £1,800,000 was invoiced and paid under the MSA, but it does not confirm or deny that the total charges payable were £2,400,000 or that they were payable against milestones.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I note, as a matter for the Court rather than for me, that clause 14 of the Master Services Agreement appears to exclude liability for loss of profit and to cap the Supplier’s liability at the charges paid (approximately £1,800,000). If that clause is effective, it would significantly affect the recoverability of the loss-of-profit claim regardless of my quantification.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: charges paid (approximately £1,800,000)
classified_chunks.reason: The quoted text states an approximate amount for charges paid that is different from the amount specified in the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Platform licence & implementation (Phase 1 — core inventory & EPOS) — Qty: 1 — Charge (£): 1,500,000
classified_chunks.reason: This quote specifies a charge of £1,500,000 for Phase 1, which is a component of a potential total but does not confirm the proposition's stated total of £2,400,000 or the payment against milestones.

proposition_text: It was an express term of the MSA and the SOW that the go-live date for the Platform was fixed at 1 October 2024, and that time was of the essence in respect of that date.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The go-live date of 1 October 2024 is a target date. The detailed implementation plan is a planning estimate and is subject to the change control procedure in clause 3 of the MSA.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The go-live date of 1 October 2024 is a target date.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote states that the go-live date is a 'target date,' which directly contradicts the proposition that the date was 'fixed' and that 'time was of the essence.'

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The parties agree that, to accommodate the additional works, the go-live date is revised from 1 October 2024 to 18 November 2024. The revised date applies to the Platform as a whole.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the go-live date is revised from 1 October 2024 to 18 November 2024
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly states that the go-live date was changed from 1 October 2024, which contradicts the proposition that the date was fixed at 1 October 2024.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: On 2 September 2024 I signed Change Order No. 3. This added the customer loyalty module, which Helena Vance had asked TechFlow to provide and which was not in the original Statement of Work, and it revised the go-live date from 1 October to 18 November 2024. I understood at the time that we were changing both the scope and the date.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: it revised the go-live date from 1 October to 18 November 2024
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates that the go-live date of 1 October was changed, directly contradicting the proposition that the date was fixed.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Timetable and scope. The go-live date was a target, not a fixed date. It moved from 1 October to 18 November 2024 by reason of Change Order No. 3, signed by your client on 2 September 2024, which added a customer loyalty module that your client requested and which was not in the original scope. Your client’s assertion that it requested no change to scope is wrong and is contradicted by its own signed Change Order.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The go-live date was a target, not a fixed date. It moved from 1 October to 18 November 2024
classified_chunks.reason: The quoted text directly states that the go-live date was a target, not fixed, and that it moved from 1 October, which unambiguously contradicts the proposition that the go-live date was fixed at 1 October.

classified_chunks.chunk_text:
Marcus,
Following the marketing review, the board wants a customer loyalty capability included in the platform before we go live — points accrual, redemption at the till, and a member database. I appreciate this was not in the original SOW, but it is now a priority for us.
Can you scope this as a change and let us have the cost and any timetable impact? We accept there may be a knock-on to the go-live date and we are willing to discuss a revised date to accommodate it.
Helena
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: We accept there may be a knock-on to the go-live date and we are willing to discuss a revised date to accommodate it.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly contradicts the proposition by indicating a willingness to discuss and revise the go-live date, implying it was not fixed and time was not of the essence.

classified_chunks.chunk_text:
Helena,
Thanks. We’ve scoped the loyalty module. The additional cost is £180,000 and, given the additional build and test, we will need to move go-live from 1 October to 18 November 2024. I’ll issue Change Order 3 for Priya’s signature to confirm both the scope and the revised date.
Marcus
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: we will need to move go-live from 1 October to 18 November 2024
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates a proposed change to the 1 October go-live date, which directly contradicts the proposition that the date was fixed.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: In around August 2024, our marketing team and the board decided we wanted a customer loyalty capability built into the new platform. I asked TechFlow to add a loyalty module. I accept this was not in the original Statement of Work. It was documented in Change Order No. 3, which my colleague Priya Nair signed on 2 September 2024, and which also moved the go-live date to 18 November 2024.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: which also moved the go-live date to 18 November 2024
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly states that the go-live date was moved, contradicting the proposition that the go-live date was fixed at 1 October 2024.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Go-live decision. Our client expressly recommended, in writing on 24 October 2024, that go-live be deferred to January 2025 to avoid the peak trading period. Your client’s Programme Director rejected that recommendation and instructed our client to proceed on 18 November 2024, accepting the associated risk. Your client cannot now complain of the consequences of its own decision.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Our client expressly recommended, in writing on 24 October 2024, that go-live be deferred to January 2025 to avoid the peak trading period. Your client’s Programme Director rejected that recommendation and instructed our client to proceed on 18 November 2024, accepting the associated risk.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote describes discussions and decisions about deferring or proceeding with go-live on dates after 1 October 2024, which directly contradicts the proposition that 1 October 2024 was a fixed, time-of-the-essence go-live date.

proposition_text: At a meeting on or about 5 February 2024, prior to entering into the MSA, TechFlow by its Sales Director Mr Daniel Frost orally represented to Meridian that the Platform would reliably support at least 10,000 concurrent transactions ("the Representation"). Meridian entered into the MSA in reliance upon the Representation. The Representation was false: the Platform was incapable of supporting that volume.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Helena Vance, Programme Director at Meridian Retail Group plc. I make this statement from my own knowledge, save where I indicate otherwise. I had overall responsibility for the Platform implementation programme on Meridian’s side.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I had overall responsibility for the Platform implementation programme on Meridian’s side.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote establishes the witness's role and responsibility for the Platform implementation programme, which is related to the subject matter of the proposition, but it does not provide any information about the alleged representation or the platform's capabilities.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Save as varied by this Deed, the MSA remains in full force and effect. EXECUTED as a DEED by the parties on the date first above written. For Meridian:	..........................  Priya Nair, Head of Procurement For TechFlow:	..........................  Gregory Manton, Commercial Director
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Save as varied by this Deed, the MSA remains in full force and effect.
classified_chunks.reason: This clause confirms the continued validity of the Master Services Agreement (MSA), which is the contract mentioned in the proposition, but it provides no information about the alleged oral representation, its content, or its truth.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: In total Meridian paid TechFlow £1.8 million. We withheld the final two milestone payments of £300,000 each — £600,000 in total — because of the problems we experienced after go-live.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: because of the problems we experienced after go-live
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates that Meridian experienced issues with the platform after it went live, which is consistent with the proposition that the platform was incapable of supporting the promised transaction volume and the representation was false.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: This certificate confirms that User Acceptance Testing of Phase 1 of the Platform (core inventory and EPOS) was carried out between 21 October and 11 November 2024. Meridian confirms that Phase 1 has passed User Acceptance Testing and is accepted, subject to the resolution of the outstanding minor (Severity 3 and 4) items listed in the snag log appended to the UAT report. No outstanding Severity 1 or Severity 2 items were recorded at the date of this certificate.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Meridian confirms that Phase 1 has passed User Acceptance Testing and is accepted, subject to the resolution of the outstanding minor (Severity 3 and 4) items listed in the snag log appended to the UAT report.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote confirms the acceptance of a phase of the platform after UAT but does not provide information about the platform's capacity for 10,000 concurrent transactions or the alleged oral representation.

proposition_text: In breach of the MSA, TechFlow delivered the Platform late. The Platform did not go live until 18 November 2024, some seven weeks after the contractual go-live date.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: As we approached go-live, Marcus Reilly recommended in writing that we delay until January 2025, after the Christmas period. I decided that we should go live on 18 November 2024. The board was firm that we needed the platform live for peak trading, and I took the decision to proceed despite TechFlow’s advice. With hindsight, going live just before Black Friday was optimistic.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I decided that we should go live on 18 November 2024. The board was firm that we needed the platform live for peak trading, and I took the decision to proceed despite TechFlow’s advice.
classified_chunks.reason: The quote states that the claimant decided the 18 November 2024 go-live date despite TechFlow's advice to delay until January 2025, which contradicts the proposition that TechFlow was responsible for the platform being delivered late on that date.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Our client’s position is that you are in repudiatory breach of the MSA. In particular: (i) you delivered the Platform late; (ii) following go-live on 18 November 2024 the Platform suffered serious and repeated failures, including critical defects in the stock-synchronisation module; and (iii) the Platform was not of satisfactory quality nor fit for purpose. These failures occurred during our client’s most important trading period and caused substantial loss.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Our client’s position is that you are in repudiatory breach of the MSA. In particular: (i) you delivered the Platform late; (ii) following go-live on 18 November 2024
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly states the client's position that the platform was delivered late and specifies the go-live date as 18 November 2024, which directly supports the proposition's claims.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The new platform went live on 18 November 2024, immediately before our peak trading period. The first few weeks were very difficult for store teams, particularly because of stock figures being wrong at the tills on the new system.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The new platform went live on 18 November 2024
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly confirms the specific go-live date of the platform as stated in the proposition.

proposition_text: Meridian did not at any time request any change to the agreed scope of the works. All delay was caused by TechFlow’s failure to allocate adequate and competent resources to the project.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: On 2 September 2024 I signed Change Order No. 3. This added the customer loyalty module, which Helena Vance had asked TechFlow to provide and which was not in the original Statement of Work, and it revised the go-live date from 1 October to 18 November 2024. I understood at the time that we were changing both the scope and the date.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: it revised the go-live date from 1 October to 18 November 2024. I understood at the time that we were changing both the scope and the date.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates that a change in scope led to a revision of the go-live date, directly contradicting the proposition that all delay was caused by TechFlow’s failure to allocate adequate and competent resources.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I was the main day-to-day contact with TechFlow. I worked closely with their Project Director, Marcus Reilly.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I was the main day-to-day contact with TechFlow.
classified_chunks.reason: This statement identifies the witness's role as a contact person but provides no information about scope changes or causes of delay, making it inconclusive regarding the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Any change to the scope, deliverables or timetable shall be agreed in writing by both parties by means of a signed Change Order. A signed Change Order varies this Agreement to the extent stated in it.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Any change to the scope, deliverables or timetable shall be agreed in writing by both parties by means of a signed Change Order.
classified_chunks.reason: This clause outlines the contractual procedure for making changes to the scope, but it does not provide any information about whether Meridian requested changes or if any changes were made, nor does it address the cause of any delays.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Availability. The Platform was not "unavailable for more than 40% of trading hours". The most significant outage, on 25 November 2024, was caused by the failure of your client’s own network provider, Northgate Telecom, and not by the Platform.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The most significant outage, on 25 November 2024, was caused by the failure of your client’s own network provider, Northgate Telecom, and not by the Platform.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote attributes a significant outage, which could cause delay, to Meridian's network provider, directly contradicting the proposition that all delay was caused by TechFlow.

classified_chunks.chunk_text:
Helena,
Following today’s readiness review, I want to put our recommendation in writing. The core build is in good shape and Phase 1 passed the bulk of UAT, but the loyalty module added under Change Order 3 is still being hardened and we have a handful of stock-sync issues we would like to close out under controlled conditions.
Our clear recommendation is to defer go-live to mid-January 2025, after the peak trading period. Going live on 18 November, immediately before Black Friday and Christmas, carries real risk and removes our contingency. We can hold the 18 November date if Meridian instructs us to, but we would be doing so against our advice.
Please let me know how you wish to proceed.
Marcus Reilly | Project Director | TechFlow Solutions
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the loyalty module added under Change Order 3 is still being hardened
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates that a loyalty module was added to the scope of works via a Change Order, which directly contradicts the proposition that Meridian did not at any time request any change to the agreed scope of the works.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Priya Nair, Head of Procurement at Meridian Retail Group plc. I make this statement from my own knowledge. I was responsible for the contractual and commercial administration of the TechFlow contract.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I was responsible for the contractual and commercial administration of the TechFlow contract.
classified_chunks.reason: The quote establishes the witness's role in relation to the contract but does not contain any information about scope changes or causes of delay.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I considered that the platform we were given was not what we had paid for, and that is why Meridian decided to bring the relationship to an end.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the platform we were given was not what we had paid for
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly asserts that TechFlow failed to deliver the agreed-upon platform, which supports the proposition that TechFlow's failures were responsible for project issues.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Save as varied by this Deed, the MSA remains in full force and effect. EXECUTED as a DEED by the parties on the date first above written. For Meridian:	..........................  Priya Nair, Head of Procurement For TechFlow:	..........................  Gregory Manton, Commercial Director
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Save as varied by this Deed
classified_chunks.reason: This clause explicitly states that the original agreement has been varied, which directly contradicts the proposition that Meridian did not at any time request any change to the agreed scope of the works.

proposition_text: On numerous occasions prior to go-live, Meridian warned TechFlow that the Platform was not ready and requested that go-live be deferred. TechFlow ignored those warnings and proceeded to go-live on 18 November 2024.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Go-live decision. Our client expressly recommended, in writing on 24 October 2024, that go-live be deferred to January 2025 to avoid the peak trading period. Your client’s Programme Director rejected that recommendation and instructed our client to proceed on 18 November 2024, accepting the associated risk. Your client cannot now complain of the consequences of its own decision.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Our client expressly recommended, in writing on 24 October 2024, that go-live be deferred to January 2025 to avoid the peak trading period. Your client’s Programme Director rejected that recommendation and instructed our client to proceed on 18 November 2024, accepting the associated risk.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote states that TechFlow (our client) recommended deferral and Meridian (your client) rejected it and instructed TechFlow to proceed, which directly contradicts the proposition that Meridian warned TechFlow and TechFlow ignored those warnings and proceeded.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: As we approached go-live, Marcus Reilly recommended in writing that we delay until January 2025, after the Christmas period. I decided that we should go live on 18 November 2024. The board was firm that we needed the platform live for peak trading, and I took the decision to proceed despite TechFlow’s advice. With hindsight, going live just before Black Friday was optimistic.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I took the decision to proceed despite TechFlow’s advice.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates that Meridian proceeded with go-live despite TechFlow advising against it, which directly contradicts the proposition that TechFlow ignored warnings and proceeded to go-live.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: On 2 September 2024 I signed Change Order No. 3. This added the customer loyalty module, which Helena Vance had asked TechFlow to provide and which was not in the original Statement of Work, and it revised the go-live date from 1 October to 18 November 2024. I understood at the time that we were changing both the scope and the date.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: it revised the go-live date from 1 October to 18 November 2024
classified_chunks.reason: This quote states that the go-live date was formally revised to 18 November, which directly contradicts the proposition's claim that TechFlow ignored requests to defer the go-live date.

proposition_text: Following go-live, the Platform was unavailable for more than 40% of trading hours during November and December 2024, causing widespread till failures and an inability to process sales across Meridian’s store estate.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Availability. The Platform was not "unavailable for more than 40% of trading hours". The most significant outage, on 25 November 2024, was caused by the failure of your client’s own network provider, Northgate Telecom, and not by the Platform.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The Platform was not "unavailable for more than 40% of trading hours".
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly refutes the proposition's claim that the Platform was unavailable for more than 40% of trading hours.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: In summary: (a) the Platform contained genuine and serious stock-synchronisation defects which fell below the expected standard; but (b) the pleaded allegation that the Platform was unavailable for more than 40% of trading hours is not supported by the evidence, and the largest single outage was caused by the Claimant’s own network provider rather than the Platform.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the pleaded allegation that the Platform was unavailable for more than 40% of trading hours is not supported by the evidence
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly states that the evidence does not support the proposition's claim regarding the platform's unavailability for more than 40% of trading hours.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Our client’s position is that you are in repudiatory breach of the MSA. In particular: (i) you delivered the Platform late; (ii) following go-live on 18 November 2024 the Platform suffered serious and repeated failures, including critical defects in the stock-synchronisation module; and (iii) the Platform was not of satisfactory quality nor fit for purpose. These failures occurred during our client’s most important trading period and caused substantial loss.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: following go-live on 18 November 2024 the Platform suffered serious and repeated failures... These failures occurred during our client’s most important trading period and caused substantial loss.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly asserts that the platform experienced serious and repeated failures after its go-live in November 2024, causing substantial loss during a critical trading period, which directly supports the proposition that the platform was unavailable and caused issues during that time.

classified_chunks.chunk_text:
Marcus,
Thank you, but the board has been clear that we need the new platform live for peak trading — that is the whole point of doing this now. Missing Black Friday and Christmas is not acceptable commercially.
Please proceed to go-live on 18 November 2024 as planned. I understand your team’s reservations and I am content to take that risk on Meridian’s side. Let’s get the remaining stock-sync items onto the snag list and press on.
Helena Vance | Programme Director | Meridian Retail Group
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I understand your team’s reservations and I am content to take that risk on Meridian’s side.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates that there were known risks associated with the go-live, but it does not provide information about the platform's actual performance or unavailability after the go-live date.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I was provided with the Platform application and database logs, the network monitoring data and the defect log for the period 18 November 2024 to 10 January 2025. Having analysed the logs, my opinion is that the total Platform unavailability attributable to the Platform itself was approximately 6.2% of trading hours over the period.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: my opinion is that the total Platform unavailability attributable to the Platform itself was approximately 6.2% of trading hours over the period.
classified_chunks.reason: The expert's opinion states the platform was unavailable for approximately 6.2% of trading hours, which directly contradicts the proposition's claim of more than 40% unavailability.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: That figure is materially lower than the figure of "more than 40% of trading hours" referred to in the pleadings. I have found no support in the logs for a figure of that order.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: That figure is materially lower than the figure of "more than 40% of trading hours" referred to in the pleadings. I have found no support in the logs for a figure of that order.
classified_chunks.reason: The expert report directly states that the actual unavailability figure is materially lower than 40% and that logs do not support the pleaded figure.

proposition_text: The Platform contained numerous defects, including critical (Severity 1) failures in the stock-synchronisation module which caused stores to display and sell against incorrect stock figures. By reason of those defects the Platform was not of satisfactory quality and was not reasonably fit for its purpose.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Notwithstanding the above, my opinion is that the Platform did contain genuine and serious defects in the stock-synchronisation module (defects D-001 and D-002 in the log). These were correctly categorised as Severity 1. They caused incorrect stock figures at the point of sale and overselling on certain lines, and persisted for approximately six weeks before being resolved.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the Platform did contain genuine and serious defects in the stock-synchronisation module (defects D-001 and D-002 in the log). These were correctly categorised as Severity 1. They caused incorrect stock figures at the point of sale and overselling on certain lines
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly confirms the existence of Severity 1 defects in the stock-synchronisation module and their effect of causing incorrect stock figures, which are key factual assertions of the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Our client’s position is that you are in repudiatory breach of the MSA. In particular: (i) you delivered the Platform late; (ii) following go-live on 18 November 2024 the Platform suffered serious and repeated failures, including critical defects in the stock-synchronisation module; and (iii) the Platform was not of satisfactory quality nor fit for purpose. These failures occurred during our client’s most important trading period and caused substantial loss.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the Platform suffered serious and repeated failures, including critical defects in the stock-synchronisation module; and (iii) the Platform was not of satisfactory quality nor fit for purpose.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly asserts that the Platform had critical defects in the stock-synchronisation module and was not of satisfactory quality or fit for purpose, which directly supports the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: In summary: (a) the Platform contained genuine and serious stock-synchronisation defects which fell below the expected standard; but (b) the pleaded allegation that the Platform was unavailable for more than 40% of trading hours is not supported by the evidence, and the largest single outage was caused by the Claimant’s own network provider rather than the Platform.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: the Platform contained genuine and serious stock-synchronisation defects which fell below the expected standard
classified_chunks.reason: The quote directly confirms the existence of genuine and serious stock-synchronisation defects and states they fell below the expected standard, supporting the proposition's claim of defects and unsatisfactory quality.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: D-001 (19 Nov 2024, Sev 1): Stock-sync: tills show incorrect stock counts; oversell on promotional lines. Status: Platform defect — fixed 31 Dec 2024
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: D-001 (19 Nov 2024, Sev 1): Stock-sync: tills show incorrect stock counts; oversell on promotional lines. Status: Platform defect
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly identifies a Severity 1 platform defect in the stock-synchronisation module that caused incorrect stock counts and overselling, aligning perfectly with the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: D-002 (22 Nov 2024, Sev 1): Stock-sync: overnight reconciliation job fails intermittently. Status: Platform defect — fixed 2 Jan 2025
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: D-002 (22 Nov 2024, Sev 1): Stock-sync: overnight reconciliation job fails intermittently. Status: Platform defect
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly identifies a Severity 1 platform defect within the stock-synchronisation module, which aligns with the proposition's claim of critical failures in that module.

proposition_text: Meridian did not accept the Platform or any part of it, and no acceptance or sign-off was given by Meridian at any time.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I considered that the platform we were given was not what we had paid for, and that is why Meridian decided to bring the relationship to an end.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I considered that the platform we were given was not what we had paid for, and that is why Meridian decided to bring the relationship to an end.
classified_chunks.reason: The quote expresses Meridian's dissatisfaction with the platform and their decision to end the relationship, which the provided context explicitly states is not evidence of formal acceptance or non-acceptance.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: This certificate confirms that User Acceptance Testing of Phase 1 of the Platform (core inventory and EPOS) was carried out between 21 October and 11 November 2024. Meridian confirms that Phase 1 has passed User Acceptance Testing and is accepted, subject to the resolution of the outstanding minor (Severity 3 and 4) items listed in the snag log appended to the UAT report. No outstanding Severity 1 or Severity 2 items were recorded at the date of this certificate.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Meridian confirms that Phase 1 has passed User Acceptance Testing and is accepted, subject to the resolution of the outstanding minor (Severity 3 and 4) items listed in the snag log appended to the UAT report.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly contradicts the proposition by stating that Meridian accepted Phase 1 of the Platform.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Helena Vance, Programme Director at Meridian Retail Group plc. I make this statement from my own knowledge, save where I indicate otherwise. I had overall responsibility for the Platform implementation programme on Meridian’s side.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I had overall responsibility for the Platform implementation programme on Meridian’s side.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote establishes the witness's role in the Platform implementation but does not provide any information about whether Meridian accepted or signed off on the Platform.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Meridian Retail Group plc – Master Services Agreement dated 14 March 2024 – NOTICE OF TERMINATION AND LETTER BEFORE ACTION
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: NOTICE OF TERMINATION AND LETTER BEFORE ACTION
classified_chunks.reason: This phrase indicates the termination of an agreement and the initiation of a dispute, but it does not provide information about whether the Platform was accepted or not.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: On 12 November 2024 I signed the Phase 1 Acceptance Certificate, confirming that Phase 1 of the platform had passed user acceptance testing. There were some minor snags listed, but no critical items were outstanding at that point.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: On 12 November 2024 I signed the Phase 1 Acceptance Certificate, confirming that Phase 1 of the platform had passed user acceptance testing.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly states that an Acceptance Certificate was signed, which contradicts the proposition that no acceptance or sign-off was given by Meridian.

classified_chunks.chunk_text:
Marcus,
Thank you, but the board has been clear that we need the new platform live for peak trading — that is the whole point of doing this now. Missing Black Friday and Christmas is not acceptable commercially.
Please proceed to go-live on 18 November 2024 as planned. I understand your team’s reservations and I am content to take that risk on Meridian’s side. Let’s get the remaining stock-sync items onto the snag list and press on.
Helena Vance | Programme Director | Meridian Retail Group
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Please proceed to go-live on 18 November 2024 as planned.
classified_chunks.reason: This instruction from a Meridian representative to launch the platform directly contradicts the proposition that Meridian did not accept the platform or give any sign-off.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I signed the Master Services Agreement on Meridian’s behalf on 14 March 2024, and the related Order Form. The total contract value was £2.4 million.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I signed the Master Services Agreement on Meridian’s behalf on 14 March 2024, and the related Order Form.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates Meridian entered into a contract for services, but it does not address whether Meridian accepted or signed off on the Platform itself as a deliverable.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: In total Meridian paid TechFlow £1.8 million. We withheld the final two milestone payments of £300,000 each — £600,000 in total — because of the problems we experienced after go-live.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: We withheld the final two milestone payments of £300,000 each — £600,000 in total — because of the problems we experienced after go-live.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote discusses withholding payments due to post-go-live problems, which does not provide information about whether formal contractual acceptance or sign-off was given.

proposition_text: Further, TechFlow failed to provide adequate training to Meridian’s staff in the use of the Platform.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Training of the Customer’s store and head-office staff in the use of the Platform is the responsibility of the Customer. The Supplier shall provide a train-the-trainer session and written user guides only.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Training of the Customer’s store and head-office staff in the use of the Platform is the responsibility of the Customer. The Supplier shall provide a train-the-trainer session and written user guides only.
classified_chunks.reason: The quote explicitly states that the Customer is responsible for training its own staff and the Supplier's role is limited to a train-the-trainer session and user guides, directly contradicting the proposition that TechFlow failed to provide adequate training to Meridian’s staff.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: In total Meridian paid TechFlow £1.8 million. We withheld the final two milestone payments of £300,000 each — £600,000 in total — because of the problems we experienced after go-live.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: because of the problems we experienced after go-live
classified_chunks.reason: This statement indicates that payments were withheld due to general issues, which is consistent with the proposition that inadequate training was one such problem, though it does not explicitly confirm it.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Priya Nair, Head of Procurement at Meridian Retail Group plc. I make this statement from my own knowledge. I was responsible for the contractual and commercial administration of the TechFlow contract.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I was responsible for the contractual and commercial administration of the TechFlow contract.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote identifies the witness's role and her responsibility for the contract, but it does not provide any information about the training provided by TechFlow.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: This certificate confirms that User Acceptance Testing of Phase 1 of the Platform (core inventory and EPOS) was carried out between 21 October and 11 November 2024. Meridian confirms that Phase 1 has passed User Acceptance Testing and is accepted, subject to the resolution of the outstanding minor (Severity 3 and 4) items listed in the snag log appended to the UAT report. No outstanding Severity 1 or Severity 2 items were recorded at the date of this certificate.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Meridian confirms that Phase 1 has passed User Acceptance Testing and is accepted
classified_chunks.reason: The successful passing of User Acceptance Testing by Meridian suggests that their staff possessed sufficient understanding to operate the platform, which is in tension with a failure to provide adequate training.

proposition_text: By reason of the matters aforesaid Meridian has suffered loss and damage.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I considered that the platform we were given was not what we had paid for, and that is why Meridian decided to bring the relationship to an end.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I considered that the platform we were given was not what we had paid for
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly asserts that Meridian paid for a platform that was not delivered as expected, which supports the claim of wasted expenditure and thus loss and damage.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Loss. Your client’s pleaded loss of profit is grossly overstated and ignores (a) the flooding of your client’s Lutterworth distribution centre in December 2024 and (b) the well-documented downturn in the retail sector during that quarter. In any event, clause 14 of the MSA excludes liability for loss of profit and caps our client’s liability at the charges actually paid.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: caps our client’s liability at the charges actually paid
classified_chunks.reason: This statement from the defendant acknowledges the 'charges actually paid' as a potential measure for liability, which is consistent with Meridian's claim that the sums paid constitute its wasted expenditure loss.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I am Helena Vance, Programme Director at Meridian Retail Group plc. I make this statement from my own knowledge, save where I indicate otherwise. I had overall responsibility for the Platform implementation programme on Meridian’s side.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: I had overall responsibility for the Platform implementation programme on Meridian’s side.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote establishes the witness's role in the program but does not provide any information about Meridian suffering loss or damage.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Our client’s position is that you are in repudiatory breach of the MSA. In particular: (i) you delivered the Platform late; (ii) following go-live on 18 November 2024 the Platform suffered serious and repeated failures, including critical defects in the stock-synchronisation module; and (iii) the Platform was not of satisfactory quality nor fit for purpose. These failures occurred during our client’s most important trading period and caused substantial loss.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: These failures occurred during our client’s most important trading period and caused substantial loss.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote directly asserts that the failures, which are the basis for the proposition's 'matters aforesaid', caused substantial loss to Meridian.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: The pleaded wasted expenditure of £1,800,000 is consistent with the Claimant’s accounting records and the sums invoiced and paid under the Master Services Agreement. I have no reason to challenge this element on a wasted-expenditure basis.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: The pleaded wasted expenditure of £1,800,000 is consistent with the Claimant’s accounting records and the sums invoiced and paid under the Master Services Agreement. I have no reason to challenge this element on a wasted-expenditure basis.
classified_chunks.reason: The expert report directly confirms the consistency of the pleaded wasted expenditure with accounting records and states no reason to challenge it, thereby supporting the claim of loss and damage.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Our trading in December 2024 was poor, but the platform was not the only reason. Our distribution centre at Lutterworth flooded on 3 December 2024 and was effectively out of action for about ten days, which left around 40 stores short of stock on key lines in the first half of December. The market was also weak that quarter, with footfall down across the sector and heavy discounting by competitors.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Our trading in December 2024 was poor, but the platform was not the only reason.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote indicates that other factors contributed to poor trading, thereby weakening the claim that the alleged matters were the sole cause of Meridian's loss and damage.

proposition_text: By reason of the matters aforesaid Meridian has suffered loss and damage.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: I considered that the platform we were given was not what we had paid for, and that is why Meridian decided to bring the relationship to an end.

classified_chunks.reason: This statement indicates Meridian terminated a relationship, an action that could foreseeably lead to the loss and damage alleged in the proposition.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Loss. The market was also weak that quarter, with footfall down across the sector and heavy discounting by competitors.

proposition_text: Further, Meridian claims interest on such sums as may be found due, pursuant to section 35A of the Senior Courts Act 1981, at such rate and for such period as the Court thinks fit.

classified_chunks.chunk_text: Outstanding sums. Your client has wrongly withheld the final two milestone payments totalling £600,000, which remain due to our client. Our client reserves the right to counterclaim.
classified_chunks.verbatim_quote: Your client has wrongly withheld the final two milestone payments totalling £600,000, which remain due to our client.
classified_chunks.reason: This quote asserts that Meridian (referred to as 'Your client') owes money to TechFlow, directly contradicting the proposition's premise that sums are due to Meridian upon which interest could be claimed.
